member.name: M:NanoXLSX.BasicFormulas.GetVLookup(NanoXLSX.Worksheet,NanoXLSX.Address,System.Object,NanoXLSX.Worksheet,NanoXLSX.Range,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Function to generate a Vlookup as Excel function
member.param: Target worksheet of the query argument. Can be null if on the same worksheet
In case of a reference lookup, query address of a cell as string
In case of a numeric lookup, number for the lookup
Target worksheet of the matrix. Can be null if on the same worksheet
Column index of the target column (1 based)
If true, an exact match is applied to the lookup
If true, the lookup is a numeric lookup, otherwise a reference lookup
member.returns: Prepared Cell object, ready to be added to a worksheet

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Cell.SetCellLockedState(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets the lock state of the cell
member.param: If true, the cell will be locked if the worksheet is protected
If true, the value of the cell will be invisible if the worksheet is protected

member.remarks: The listed exception should never happen because the mentioned style is internally generated

member.summary: Sets the style of the cell
member.returns: If the passed style already exists in the workbook, the existing one will be returned, otherwise the passed one
member.exception: Throws an StyleException if the style cannot be referenced or no style was defined

Throws an RangeException if the range is out of range (A-XFD and 1 to 1048576)

member.summary: The import options define global rules to import worksheets. The options are mainly to override particular cell types (e.g. interpretation of dates as numbers)

member.summary: Column types to enforce during the import

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.ColumnType.Numeric
member.summary: Cells are tried to be imported as numbers (double)

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.ColumnType.Date
member.summary: Cells are tried to be imported as dates (DateTime)

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.ColumnType.Time
member.summary: Cells are tried to be imported as times (TimeSpan)

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.ColumnType.Bool
member.summary: Cells are tried to be imported as bools

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.ColumnType.String
member.summary: Cells are all imported as strings, using the ToString() method

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.EnforceDateTimesAsNumbers
member.summary: If true, date or time values (default format number 14 or 21) will be interpreted as numeric values globally. This option overrules possible column options, defined by .

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.EnforcedColumnTypes
member.summary: Type enforcing rules during import for particular columns

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.ImportOptions.EnforcingStartRowNumber
member.summary: The row number (zero-based) where enforcing rules are started to be applied. This is, for instance, to prevent enforcing in a header row

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.XlsxWriter.Save
member.summary: Method to save the workbook
member.exception: Throws IOException in case of an error
Throws an OutOfRangeException if the start or end address of a handled cell range was out of range
Throws a FormatException if a handled date cannot be translated to (Excel internal) OADate
Throws an StyleException if one of the styles of the workbook cannot be referenced or is null
member.remarks: The StyleException should never happen in this state if the internally managed style collection was not tampered.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.XlsxWriter.SaveAsync
member.summary: Method to save the workbook asynchronous.
member.remarks: Possible Exceptions are IOException, RangeException, and StyleException. These exceptions may not emerge directly if using the async method since async/await adds further abstraction layers.
member.returns: Async Task

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.XlsxWriter.SaveAsStream(System.IO.Stream,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Method to save the workbook as stream
member.param: Optional parameter to keep the stream open after writing (used for MemoryStreams; default is false)
member.exception: Throws IOException in case of an error
Throws an OutOfRangeException if the start or end address of a handled cell range was out of range
Throws a FormatException if a handled date cannot be translated to (Excel internal) OADate
Throws an StyleException if one of the styles of the workbook cannot be referenced or is null
member.remarks: The StyleException should never happen in this state if the internally managed style collection was not tampered.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.XlsxWriter.SaveAsStreamAsync(System.IO.Stream,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Method to save the workbook as stream asynchronous.
member.param: Optional parameter to keep the stream open after writing (used for MemoryStreams; default is false)
member.remarks: Possible Exceptions are IOException, RangeException, and StyleException. These exceptions may not emerge directly if using the async method since async/await adds further abstraction layers.
member.returns: Async Task

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.XlsxWriter.EscapeXmlChars(System.String)
member.summary: Method to escape XML characters between two XML tags
member.returns: Escaped string
member.remarks: Note: The XML specs allow characters up to the character value of 0x10FFFF. However, the C# char range is only up to 0xFFFF. NanoXLSX will neglect all values above this level in the sanitizing check. Illegal characters like 0x1 will be replaced with a white space (0x20)

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.XlsxWriter.EscapeXmlAttributeChars(System.String)
member.summary: Method to escape XML characters in an XML attribute
member.returns: Escaped string

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.XlsxWriter.GeneratePasswordHash(System.String)
member.summary:
Method to generate an Excel internal password hash to protect workbooks or worksheets
This method is derived from the c++ implementation by Kohei Yoshida (http://kohei.us/2008/01/18/excel-sheet-protection-password-hash/)
member.remarks: WARNING! Do not use this method to encrypt 'real' passwords or data outside from NanoXLSX. This is only a minor security feature. Use a proper cryptography method instead.
member.param: Password string in UTF-8 to encrypt
member.returns: 16 bit hash as hex string

member.name: T:NanoXLSX.LowLevel.NamespaceDoc
member.summary: Sub-namespace with all low-level classes and functions. This namespace is necessary to read and generate files but should not be used as pat of the API. Use the classes and functions in the namespace NanoXLSX instead

member.name: T:NanoXLSX.Metadata
member.summary: Class representing the meta data of a workbook

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Application
member.summary: Gets or sets the application which created the workbook. Default is NanoXLSX

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.ApplicationVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the version of the creation application. Default is the library version of NanoXLSX. Use the format xxxxx.yyyyy (e.g. 1.0 or 55.9875) in case of a custom value.

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Category
member.summary: Gets or sets the category of the document. There are no predefined values or restrictions about the content of this field

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Company
member.summary: Gets or sets the company owning the document. This value is for organizational purpose. Add more than one manager by using the semicolon (;) between the words

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.ContentStatus
member.summary: Gets or sets the status of the document. There are no predefined values or restrictions about the content of this field

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Creator
member.summary: Gets or sets the creator of the workbook. Add more than one creator by using the semicolon (;) between the authors

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets the description of the document or comment about it

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.HyperlinkBase
member.summary: Gets or sets the hyper-link base of the document.

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Keywords
member.summary: Gets or sets the keywords of the workbook. Separate particular keywords with semicolons (;)

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Manager
member.summary: Gets or sets the responsible manager of the document. This value is for organizational purpose.

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Subject
member.summary: Gets or sets the subject of the workbook

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.Title
member.summary: Gets or sets the title of the workbook

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Metadata.UseColorMRU
member.summary: Gets or sets the whether custom defined colors (in styles) will be added as recent colors (MRU). If true. MRU information will be added

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Metadata.#ctor
member.summary: Default constructor

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Metadata.CheckVersion
member.summary: Checks the format of the passed version string
member.exception: Throws a FormatException if the version string is malformed

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Metadata.ParseVersion(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Method to parse a common version (major.minor.revision.build) into the compatible format (major.minor). The minimum value is 0.0 and the maximum value is 99999.99999
The minor, revision and build number are joined if possible. If the number is too long, the additional characters will be removed from the right side down to five characters (e.g. 785563 will be 78556)
member.returns: Formatted version number (e.g. 1.0 or 55.987)
member.exception: Throws a FormatException if the major number is too long or one of the numbers is negative

member.name: T:NanoXLSX.Shortener
member.summary: Class to provide access to the current worksheet with a shortened syntax. Note: The WS object can be null if the workbook was created without a worksheet. The object will be available as soon as the current worksheet is defined

member.summary: Sets the worksheet accessed by the shortener

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Shortener.Value(System.Object)
member.summary: Sets a value into the current cell and moves the cursor to the next cell (column or row depending on the defined cell direction)
member.exception: Throws a WorksheetException if no worksheet was defined

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Shortener.Value(System.Object,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Sets a value with style into the current cell and moves the cursor to the next cell (column or row depending on the defined cell direction)
member.exception: Throws a WorksheetException if no worksheet was defined

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Shortener.Formula(System.String)
member.summary: Sets a formula into the current cell and moves the cursor to the next cell (column or row depending on the defined cell direction)
member.exception: Throws a WorksheetException if no worksheet was defined

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Shortener.Formula(System.String,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Sets a formula with style into the current cell and moves the cursor to the next cell (column or row depending on the defined cell direction)
member.exception: Throws a WorksheetException if no worksheet was defined

member.summary: Moves the cursor one row down

member.summary: Moves the cursor the number of defined rows down

member.name: T:NanoXLSX.Utils
member.summary: General Utils class with static methods

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.Utils.MIN_OADATE_VALUE
member.summary: Minimum valid OAdate value (1900-01-01)

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.Utils.MAX_OADATE_VALUE
member.summary: Maximum valid OAdate value (9999-12-31)

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.Utils.FIRST_VALID_EXCEL_DATE
member.summary:
All dates before this date are shifted in Excel by -1.0, since Excel assumes wrongly that the year 1900 is a leap year.
See also: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/office/troubleshoot/excel/wrongly-assumes-1900-is-leap-year

member.name: F:NanoXLSX.Utils.INVARIANT_CULTURE
member.summary: Constant for number conversion. The invariant culture (represents mostly the US numbering scheme) ensures that no culture-specific punctuations are used when converting numbers to strings, This is especially important for OOXML number values. See also: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.globalization.cultureinfo.invariantculture?view=net-5.0

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.GetOADateTimeString(System.DateTime,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Method to convert a date or date and time into the internal Excel time format (OAdate)
member.returns: Date or date and time as number
member.exception: Throws a FormatException if the passed date cannot be translated to the OADate format
member.remarks:
Excel assumes wrongly that the year 1900 is a leap year. There is a gap of 1.0 between 1900-02-28 and 1900-03-01. This method corrects all dates from the first valid date (1900-01-01) to 1900-03-01. However, Excel displays the minimum valid date as 1900-01-00, although 0 is not a valid description for a day of month. In conformance to the OAdate specifications, the maximum valid date is 9999-12-31 23:59:59 (plus 999 milliseconds).
See also: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.datetime.tooadate?view=netcore-3.1
See also: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/office/troubleshoot/excel/wrongly-assumes-1900-is-leap-year

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.GetOATimeString(System.TimeSpan,System.IFormatProvider)
member.summary: Method to convert a time into the internal Excel time format (OAdate without days)
member.param: Time to process. The date component of the timespan is neglected
CultureInfo for proper formatting of the decimal point
member.returns: Time as number
member.exception: Throws a FormatException if the passed timespan is invalid
member.remarks: The time is represented by a OAdate without the date component. A time range is between >0.0 (00:00:00) and <1.0 (23:59:59)

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.ToUpper(System.String)
member.summary: Transforms a string to upper case with null check and invariant culture
member.returns: Upper case string

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.ToString(System.Int32)
member.summary: Transforms an integer to an invariant sting
member.returns: Integer as string

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.GetInternalColumnWidth(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Calculates the internal width of a column in characters. This width is used only in the XML documents of worksheets and is usually not exposed to the (Excel) end user
member.remarks:
The internal width deviates slightly from the column width, entered in Excel. Although internal, the default column width of 10 characters is visible in Excel as 10.71. The deviation depends on the maximum digit width of the default font, as well as its text padding and various constants.
In case of the width 10.0 and the default digit width 7.0, as well as the padding 5.0 of the default font Calibri (size 11), the internal width is approximately 10.7142857 (rounded to 10.71).
Note that the column hight is not affected by this consideration. The entered height in Excel is the actual height in the worksheet XML documents.
This method is derived from the Perl implementation by John McNamara (https://stackoverflow.com/a/5010899)
See also: ECMA-376, Part 1, Chapter 18.3.1.13
member.param: Maximum digit with of the default font (default is 7.0 for Calibri, size 11)
Text padding of the default font (default is 5.0 for Calibri, size 11)
member.returns: The internal column width in characters, used in worksheet XML documents

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.GetInternalRowHeight(System.Single)
member.summary: Calculates the internal height of a row. This height is used only in the XML documents of worksheets and is usually not exposed to the (Excel) end user
member.remarks:
The height is based on the calculated amount of pixels. One point are ~1.333 (1+1/3) pixels. After the conversion, the number of pixels is rounded to the nearest integer and calculated back to points.
Therefore, the originally defined row height will slightly deviate, based on this pixel snap
member.returns: The internal row height which snaps to the nearest pixel

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.GetInternalPaneSplitWidth(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Calculates the internal width of a split pane in a worksheet. This width is used only in the XML documents of worksheets and is not exposed to the (Excel) end user
member.remarks:
The internal split width is based on the width of one or more columns. It also depends on the maximum digit width of the default font, as well as its text padding and various constants.
See also for additional details.
This method is derived from the Perl implementation by John McNamara (https://stackoverflow.com/a/5010899)
See also: ECMA-376, Part 1, Chapter 18.3.1.13
The three optional parameters maxDigitWidth and textPadding probably don't have to be changed ever.
member.param: Target column(s) width (one or more columns, displayed in Excel)
Maximum digit with of the default font (default is 7.0 for Calibri, size 11)
Text padding of the default font (default is 5.0 for Calibri, size 11)
member.returns: The internal pane width, used in worksheet XML documents in case of worksheet splitting

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Utils.GetInternalPaneSplitHeight(System.Single)
member.summary: Calculates the internal height of a split pane in a worksheet. This height is used only in the XML documents of worksheets and is not exposed to the (Excel) user
member.remarks:
The internal split height is based on the height of one or more rows. It also depends on various constants.
This method is derived from the Perl implementation by John McNamara (https://stackoverflow.com/a/5010899)
member.param: Target row(s) height (one or more rows, displayed in Excel)
member.returns: The internal pane height, used in worksheet XML documents in case of worksheet splitting

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.ResolveMergedCells
member.summary: Method to resolve all merged cells in all worksheets. Only the value of the very first cell of the locked cells range will be visible. The other values are still present (set to EMPTY) but will not be stored in the worksheet.
member.exception: Throws a StyleException if one of the styles of the merged cells cannot be referenced or is null

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.Save
member.summary: Saves the workbook
member.exception: Throws IOException in case of an error
Throws a RangeException if the start or end address of a handled cell range was out of range
Throws a FormatException if a handled date cannot be translated to (Excel internal) OADate
Throws a StyleException if one of the styles of the workbook cannot be referenced or is null

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.SaveAsync
member.summary: Saves the workbook asynchronous.
member.returns: Task object (void)
member.exception: May throw an IOException in case of an error. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.
May throw a RangeException if the start or end address of a handled cell range was out of range. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.
May throw a FormatException if a handled date cannot be translated to (Excel internal) OADate. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.
May throw a StyleException if one of the styles of the workbook cannot be referenced or is null. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.SaveAs(System.String)
member.summary: Saves the workbook with the defined name
member.exception: Throws IOException in case of an error
Throws a RangeException if the start or end address of a handled cell range was out of range
Throws a FormatException if a handled date cannot be translated to (Excel internal) OADate
Throws a StyleException if one of the styles of the workbook cannot be referenced or is null

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.SaveAsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Saves the workbook with the defined name asynchronous.
member.returns: Task object (void)
member.exception: May throw an IOException in case of an error. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.
May throw a RangeException if the start or end address of a handled cell range was out of range. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.
May throw a FormatException if a handled date cannot be translated to (Excel internal) OADate. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.
May throw a StyleException if one of the styles of the workbook cannot be referenced or is null. The asynchronous operation may hide the exception.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.SetCurrentWorksheet(System.String)

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.SetSelectedWorksheet(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the selected worksheet in the output workbook
member.remarks: This method does not set the current worksheet while design time. Use SetCurrentWorksheet instead for this
member.exception: Throws a RangeException if the index of the worksheet is out of range
Throws a WorksheetException if the worksheet to be set selected is hidden

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.SetWorkbookProtection(System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Sets or removes the workbook protection. If protectWindows and protectStructure are both false, the workbook will not be protected
member.param: If true, the workbook will be protected, otherwise not
If true, the windows will be locked if the workbook is protected
If true, the structure will be locked if the workbook is protected
Optional password. If null or empty, no password will be set in case of protection

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.SetSelectedWorksheet(NanoXLSX.Worksheet)
member.summary: Sets the selected worksheet in the output workbook
member.remarks: This method does not set the current worksheet while design time. Use SetCurrentWorksheet instead for this
member.param: Worksheet object (must be in the collection of worksheets)
member.exception: Throws a WorksheetException if the worksheet was not found in the worksheet collection or if it is hidden

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.ValidateWorksheets
member.summary:
Validates the worksheets regarding several conditions that must be met:
- At least one worksheet must be defined
- A hidden worksheet cannot be the selected one
- At least one worksheet must be visible
If one of the conditions is not met, an exception is thrown

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Workbook.Load(System.String,NanoXLSX.ImportOptions)
member.summary: Loads a workbook from a file
member.param: Import options to override the data types of columns. These options can be used to cope with wrong interpreted data, caused by irregular styles
member.returns: Workbook object
member.exception: Throws IOException in case of an error

members.member:

member.summary: Gets the list of SheetProtectionValues. These values define the allowed actions if the worksheet is protected

member.summary: Gets or sets whether the worksheet is protected. If true, protection is enabled

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.WorkbookReference
member.summary: Gets or sets the Reference to the parent Workbook

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.Hidden
member.summary:
Gets or sets whether the worksheet is hidden. If true, the worksheet is not listed as tab in the workbook's worksheet selection
If the worksheet is not part of a workbook, or the only one in the workbook, an exception will be thrown.
If the worksheet is the selected one, and attempted to set hidden, an exception will be thrown. Define another selected worksheet prior to this call, in this case.

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.PaneSplitTopHeight
member.summary:
Gets the height of the upper, horizontal split pane, measured from the top of the window.
The value is nullable. If null, no horizontal split of the worksheet is applied.
The value is only applicable to split the worksheet into panes, but not to freeze them.
See also:
member.remarks: Note: This value will be modified to the Excel-internal representation, calculated by .

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.PaneSplitLeftWidth
member.summary:
Gets the width of the left, vertical split pane, measured from the left of the window.
The value is nullable. If null, no vertical split of the worksheet is applied
The value is only applicable to split the worksheet into panes, but not to freeze them.
See also:
member.remarks: Note: This value will be modified to the Excel-internal representation, calculated by .

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.FreezeSplitPanes
member.summary:
Gets whether split panes are frozen.
The value is nullable. If null, no freezing is applied. This property also does not apply if is null

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.PaneSplitTopLeftCell
member.summary:
Gets the Top Left cell address of the bottom right pane if applicable and splitting is applied.
The column is only relevant for vertical split, whereas the row component is only relevant for a horizontal split.
The value is nullable. If null, no splitting was defined.

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.PaneSplitAddress
member.summary:
Gets the split address for frozen panes or if pane split was defined in number of columns and / or rows.
For vertical splits, only the column component is considered. For horizontal splits, only the row component is considered.
The value is nullable. If null, no frozen panes or split by columns / rows are applied to the worksheet. However, splitting can still be applied, if the value is defined in characters.
See also: and for splitting in characters (without freezing)

member.name: P:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.ActivePane
member.summary:
Gets the active Pane is splitting is applied.
The value is nullable. If null, no splitting was defined

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.#ctor
member.summary: Default Constructor

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32,NanoXLSX.Workbook)
member.summary: Constructor with name and sheet ID
member.param: ID of the worksheet (for internal use)

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.AddNextCell(System.Object)
member.summary: Adds an object to the next cell position. If the type of the value does not match with one of the supported data types, it will be casted to a String. A prepared object of the type Cell will not be casted but adjusted
member.remarks: Recognized are the following data types: Cell (prepared object), string, int, double, float, long, DateTime, TimeSpan, bool. All other types will be casted into a string using the default ToString() method
member.exception: Throws a RangeException if the next cell is out of range (on row or column)

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.AddNextCell(System.Object,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Adds an object to the next cell position. If the type of the value does not match with one of the supported data types, it will be casted to a String. A prepared object of the type Cell will not be casted but adjusted
member.remarks: Recognized are the following data types: Cell (prepared object), string, int, double, float, long, DateTime, TimeSpan, bool. All other types will be casted into a string using the default ToString() method
member.param: Style object to apply on this cell
member.exception: Throws a RangeException if the next cell is out of range (on row or column)
Throws a StyleException if the default style was malformed

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.AddNextCell(NanoXLSX.Cell,System.Boolean,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Method to insert a generic cell to the next cell position
member.param: If true, the address value (row or column) will be incremented, otherwise not
If not null, the defined style will be applied to the cell, otherwise no style or the default style will be applied
member.remarks: Recognized are the following data types: string, int, double, float, long, DateTime, TimeSpan, bool. All other types will be casted into a string using the default ToString() method
member.exception: Throws a StyleException if the default style was malformed or if the active style cannot be referenced

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.CastValue(System.Object,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Method to cast a value or align an object of the type Cell to the context of the worksheet
member.param: Unspecified value or object of the type Cell
member.returns: Cell object

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.AddCell(System.Object,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds an object to the defined cell address. If the type of the value does not match with one of the supported data types, it will be casted to a String. A prepared object of the type Cell will not be casted but adjusted
member.remarks: Recognized are the following data types: Cell (prepared object), string, int, double, float, long, DateTime, TimeSpan, bool. All other types will be casted into a string using the default ToString() method
member.exception: Throws an UndefinedStyleException if the active style cannot be referenced while creating the cell
Throws an RangeException if the passed cell address is out of range

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.AddCell(System.Object,System.Int32,System.Int32,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Adds an object to the defined cell address. If the type of the value does not match with one of the supported data types, it will be casted to a String. A prepared object of the type Cell will not be casted but adjusted
member.param: Style to apply on the cell
member.remarks: Recognized are the following data types: Cell (prepared object), string, int, double, float, long, DateTime, TimeSpan, bool. All other types will be casted into a string using the default ToString() method
member.exception: Throws an UndefinedStyleException if the passed style is malformed
Throws an RangeException if the passed cell address is out of range

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.AddCell(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Adds an object to the defined cell address. If the type of the value does not match with one of the supported data types, it will be casted to a String. A prepared object of the type Cell will not be casted but adjusted
member.param: Cell address in the format A1 - XFD1048576
member.remarks: Recognized are the following data types: Cell (prepared object), string, int, double, float, long, DateTime, TimeSpan, bool. All other types will be casted into a string using the default ToString() method
member.exception: Throws an UndefinedStyleException if the active style cannot be referenced while creating the cell
Throws an RangeException if the passed cell address is out of range

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.SetStyle(NanoXLSX.Range,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Sets the passed style on the passed cell range. If cells are already existing, the style will be added or replaced. Otherwise, an empty (numeric) cell will be added with the assigned style
member.param: Cell range to apply the style
member.remarks: Note: This method may invalidate an existing date value since dates are defined by specific style. The result of a redefinition will be a number, instead of a date

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.SetStyle(NanoXLSX.Address,NanoXLSX.Address,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)

member.param: Start address of the cell range
End address of the cell range
member.remarks: Note: This method may invalidate an existing date value since dates are defined by specific style. The result of a redefinition will be a number, instead of a date

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.SetStyle(NanoXLSX.Address,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Sets the passed style on the passed (singular) cell address. If the cell is already existing, the style will be added or replaced. Otherwise, an empty (numeric) cell will be added with the assigned style
member.param: Cell address to apply the style
member.remarks: Note: This method may invalidate an existing date value since dates are defined by specific style. The result of a redefinition will be a number, instead of a date

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.SetStyle(System.String,NanoXLSX.Styles.Style)
member.summary: Sets the passed style on the passed address expression. Such an expression may be a single cell or a cell range. If the cell is already existing, the style will be added or replaced. Otherwise, an empty (numeric) cell or cell range will be added with the assigned style
member.param: Expression of a cell address or range of addresses
member.remarks: Note: This method may invalidate an existing date value since dates are defined by specific style. The result of a redefinition will be a number, instead of a date

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetLastColumnNumber
member.summary: Gets the last existing column number in the current worksheet (zero-based)
member.returns: Zero-based column number. In case of a empty worksheet, -1 will be returned
member.remarks: GetLastColumnNumber() will not return the last column with data in any case. If there is a formated but empty cell (or many) beyond the last cell with data, GetLastColumnNumber() will return the column number of this empty cell. Use in this case.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetLastDataColumnNumber
member.summary: Gets the last existing column number with data in the current worksheet (zero-based)
member.returns: Zero-based column number. In case of a empty worksheet, -1 will be returned
member.remarks: GetLastDataColumnNumber() will ignore formatted but empty cells beyond the last column with data. If you want the last defined column, use instead.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetLastRowNumber
member.summary: Gets the last existing row number in the current worksheet (zero-based)
member.returns: Zero-based row number. In case of a empty worksheet, -1 will be returned
member.remarks: GetLastRowNumber() will not return the last row with data in any case. If there is a formated but empty cell (or many) beyond the last cell with data, GetLastRowNumber() will return the row number of this empty cell. Use in this case.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetLastDataRowNumber
member.summary: Gets the last existing row number with data in the current worksheet (zero-based)
member.returns: Zero-based row number. In case of a empty worksheet, -1 will be returned
member.remarks: GetLastDataColumnNumber() will ignore formatted but empty cells beyond the last column with data. If you want the last defined column, use instead.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetLastCellAddress
member.summary: Gets the last existing cell in the current worksheet (bottom right)
member.returns: Cell Address
member.remarks: GetLastCellAddress() will not return the last cell with data in any case. If there is a formated but empty cell (or many) beyond the last cell with data, GetLastCellAddress() will return the address of this empty cell. Use in this case.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetLastDataCellAddress
member.summary: Gets the last existing cell with data in the current worksheet (bottom right)
member.returns: Cell Address
member.remarks: GetLastDataCellAddress() will ignore formatted but empty cells beyond the last cell with data. If you want the last defined cell, use instead.

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetLastAddress(System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets the last existing row or column number of the current worksheet (zero-based)
member.param: If true, the output will be the last column, otherwise the last row
If true, empty cells are ignored and the last column or row is this one with a value
member.returns: Last row or column number (zero-based)

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.SanitizeWorksheetName(System.String,NanoXLSX.Workbook)
member.summary: Sanitizes a worksheet name
member.returns: Name of the sanitized worksheet

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.GetUnusedWorksheetName(System.String,NanoXLSX.Workbook)
member.summary: Determines the next unused worksheet name in the passed workbook
member.param: Original name to start the check
Workbook to look for existing worksheets
member.returns: Not yet used worksheet name
member.remarks: The 'rare' case where 10^31 Worksheets exists (leads to a crash) is deliberately not handled, since such a number of sheets would consume at least a quintillion bytes of RAM... what is vastly out of the 64 bit range

member.name: M:NanoXLSX.Worksheet.WorksheetExists(System.String,NanoXLSX.Workbook)
member.summary: Checks whether a worksheet with the given name exists
member.returns: True if the name exits, otherwise false
